title: mesosphaerum saxatile
intro: Mesosphaerum saxatile, also known as Mexican tea or Mexican mint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic effects. The plant's health benefits include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. Mesosphaerum saxatile can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, or it can be used in tinctures, capsules, or topical creams, offering a versatile range of herbal preparations.
